meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop spinning in tutorial hell. Learn how to implement actionable analytics in your SaaS MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive revenue growth.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start measuring. By integrating event-based analytics into your MVP, you transition from guessing to data-driven product iteration. Using Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you can deploy a robust tracking system in minutes, ensuring every feature serves your bottom line.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks consuming endless 'build a SaaS' tutorials, yet your repository remains a graveyard of half-baked features. The cycle is addictive, providing the illusion of progress while your actual product collects digital dust. You are trapped in the comfort of learning without the friction of shipping.

Real founders don't optimize for knowledge; they optimize for market feedback. To break the cycle, you must stop building features in a vacuum and start instrumenting your app. Analytics isn't just about tracking clicks; it is the heartbeat of your business, telling you exactly where users drop off and where your revenue leaks are hidden.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single event that signifies user success. This prevents 'vanity metric' bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Review my current MVP core loop and identify the 3 most critical user events that correlate with revenue. Suggest a lean event schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Analyze my codebase and propose a minimal event tracking schema based on the user conversion funnel.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: Standardize your tracking implementation to avoid vendor lock-in and messy manual event calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular Analytics wrapper service in TypeScript that supports PostHog/Mixpanel initialization and exposes a single trackEvent method.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a service/analytics.ts file with a singleton pattern for tracking.'

steps.title: Instrument Key Conversion Points
steps.description: Inject tracking calls into your signup, onboarding, and checkout flows to measure user journey friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the primary CTA buttons in my React components and wrap them with the analytics service trackEvent method.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Add trackEvent calls to the SignupForm and CheckoutButton components using the analytics service.'

steps.title: Automate User Context Enrichment
steps.description: Ensure every event carries user metadata, allowing you to segment data by plan type or acquisition channel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update the analytics service to automatically append user ID and session context to every tracked event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Refactor analytics.ts to include global user context middleware.'

steps.title: Validate Event Flow
steps.description: Verify that data is flowing correctly from the client to your dashboard to ensure accuracy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script to simulate a user signup flow and verify that the analytics event is captured in the console.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a test file to verify event dispatching logic.'

pro_tips: Never track everything. If you measure everything, you measure nothing. Focus on the funnel.
Use anonymous IDs for pre-signup tracking to understand your lead conversion rate.
Implement server-side tracking for payment events to prevent ad-blockers from missing revenue signals.
Set up alerts for conversion drops; knowing a bug broke your signup flow is worth more than a dashboard view.
Always include a 'timestamp' and 'source' field in your event payload for easier debugging in data warehouses.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours observing user behavior without changing the code. Identify the single biggest drop-off point and use Claude Code to run an A/B test on that specific component.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy. Now it is time to exit the tutorial cycle and start building a product that actually converts. Stop watching and start shipping today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
